title: Brief-Dev Workflow Family: the v2 pipeline as durable workflows
intention: Design-system v2 briefs become executable: an all-norn pipeline (scout → dev → verify-fix → adversarial review → harden) runs as a durable aion workflow, enriching the brief in place at every stage, composed under the live-proven stacked_dev outer arc (provision → dev → gate → human review → land), with a dispatcher above that selects briefed work from the roadmap ledger and fans it out. When this is done, 'do this roadmap item' means authoring documents and approving a DM — the workflow does the rest, every stage durable across crashes, every claim recorded next to what was actually measured. aion develops aion; the dogfood becomes the dev process.
problem: The queued work (RM-003, RM-011..014, and everything after) is currently executed by hand or through the rhai-scripted onatopp-dev-norn workflow, which dies with the process running it, trusts dev attestations as gates (its verify loop runs check+clippy but never tests or fmt), entangles review with hardening in a single graded-homework pass, and stores its execution record in workflow output instead of with the brief. Meanwhile the stacked_dev family is live-proven end to end but its inner child (onatopp_dev) is a scout-less, review-less dev loop fed unstructured document strings. Neither side speaks the v2 design system: stage outputs are ad-hoc shapes, briefs go in but never come back enriched, and nothing above a single brief exists — no dependency-ordered multi-brief dispatch.
solution: Evolve the stacked-dev family in place (no parallel family — ADR-002, ADR-008 proposed). The inner child onatopp_dev becomes brief_dev: it takes a v2 brief document plus pre-resolved context (ADR texts for the brief's design_anchor, C#/S# texts — resolution happens at dispatch time, in activities, never in deterministic workflow code), runs scout (read-only norn, deterministic session '<branch>-scout'), then dev (norn, session '<branch>', warm build concurrent via the existing tagged StartupTask fan-out), then the verify-fix loop (scoped_checks + dev_resume, cap and backoff as required inputs per ADR-001), then adversarial review+harden (norn reviewer, separate session '<branch>-review', prompt built per PROMPTING.md projection with the dev's attestation AND the measured check results), then re-verifies after any hardening. Each stage's structured report (scout-report/dev-report/review-report schemas — the codegen-subset stage contracts) is validated and appended into the brief document via the enrich_brief activity, which writes the brief file in the worktree append-only: authored fields are never touched, so the enriched record rides the same branch as the code and lands with the merge (ADR-009 proposed). The outer stacked_dev keeps its live-proven arc and contracts (request_review argv, land-from-repo-root, single review_verdict signal per ADR-006); its input reshapes to carry the v2 brief document and resolved context, and before land it calls enrich_brief once more to write the execution block (gate results, attestation, verdict, branch). A new top-level dispatch workflow takes a wave (list of brief documents + resolved context + concurrency limit, all assembled by dispatcher activities that read roadmap.json/decisions.json and refuse briefs whose depends_on have not landed), spawns stacked_dev children in dependency order — serially in the first delivery; bounded fan-out follows once parent-close (RM-001/ADR-004) lands — and returns a per-brief outcome report. Worker: the existing stacked-dev worker crate gains handlers for the new/changed activities, mirroring locals invocation-for-invocation, with wire_compat pinning every payload byte-compatible both directions. Stage-contract codecs are generated by aion codegen from schemas copied into the package's schemas/ dir, with a drift gate asserting byte-equality against the design-system canon; workflow IO and error unions stay hand-written (codegen v1 rejects unions) under wire-compat pins.

principles.text: Attestation is never the gate: agents attest, activities measure, both are recorded, divergence is review signal.
Projection, not forwarding: every stage prompt is a budgeted pure function of the enriched brief (scout ~5K chars, dev ~7K, review ~9K), unit-tested like a codec.

Expected negative outcomes are data, not errors: failed checks, failed gates, forfeited warm builds, and review-found drift are recorded results; only can't-execute conditions fail activities, and those fail terminally.
Test fixtures are captured real envelopes, never invented shapes: contract drift breaks a test, not a live run.
Deterministic workflow code never touches the filesystem or builds prompts from unresolved references: reading ledgers, resolving references, and writing enrichment are activities.
Verbatim intent rides the prompt: when the brief's roadmap item or anchored ADRs carry quotes, the agent sees the quoted words with their speaker, never a paraphrase.

goals: The family executes one of aion's own briefs end to end with real norn against the aion repo: dispatched from roadmap.json, landed on main with the enriched brief riding the merge.

Every stage payload validates against the design-system stage-contract schemas, and the stage-contract codecs are generated by aion codegen from package schemas that are byte-identical to the design-system canon (drift gate in CI).

An enriched brief on main carries: per-R# scout/dev/review blocks, the execution block with measured gate results AND the dev attestation, and untouched authored fields (verified by diffing authored fields pre/post run).

The hermetic in-process suite covers every stage outcome (scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift fixed and unfixed, harden regression caught by re-verify, gate fail, review timeout/reject/changes, land) with exclusive-PATH CLI shims emitting captured real envelopes.

The dispatch workflow executes a multi-brief wave in dependency order, refuses briefs with unlanded depends_on, and returns a per-brief outcome report; a kill -9 of the server at any point during a wave resumes without re-executing completed activities.
The dev-pipeline template ships the new family so aion new scaffolds the v2 pipeline.

non_goals.text: Parallel fan-out of briefs within a wave in the first delivery.
non_goals.reason: Lands serially first; bounded concurrency follows once parent-close (RM-001) gives the dispatcher cancellation semantics over in-flight children.
ADR-006 places quorum in the Meridian coordinator; this family keeps the single review_verdict signal it already has.

non_goals.text: Generating codecs for workflow IO and error unions.
non_goals.reason: codegen v1 rejects unions and $ref; hand-written codecs with wire-compat pins remain the proven pattern for those shapes.

non_goals.text: Replacing the rhai onatopp-dev-norn workflow in Meridian's repo.
non_goals.reason: Meridian migrates on its own schedule after the re-pin (RM-015); this cluster only retires aion's inner onatopp_dev module.

non_goals.text: Cross-repo dispatch (running this family against repos other than the one the worker sits in).
non_goals.reason: Worker placement/affinity is RM-006; the first consumer is aion itself.

Outer arc (existing): input reshape, enrichment call sites, execution block before land
Existing type module: brief document, stage report, and dispatch types join it
Existing hand-written codecs for core payloads
Existing hand-written codecs for gate/review/land

Existing activity definitions: scout, dev_review, enrich_brief join the eight
Stage prompt projection functions (P2): pure, budgeted, unit-tested
Pure append-only merge of stage reports into the brief document (authored fields untouchable)

inventory.note: Inner child today: warm_build+dev concurrent fan-out, verify-fix loop. Replaced by brief_dev.gleam (deleted, per ADR-002 — no parallel family).
Live-proven outer arc: provision → child dev → gate child → review loop (signal + timeout + caps) → land. Phase flow and contracts stay; input and enrichment call sites change.

Eight activities live-proven 2026-06-13: provision_workspace, warm_build, dev, scoped_checks, dev_resume, full_checks, request_review, land. All reused as-is except dev/dev_resume prompts now come from prompts.gleam projections.
Six workflow IO schemas exist (input/output for the three current entries); onatopp pair retired with the module, brief_dev/dispatch pairs join.
Standalone crate against the published aion-worker SDK (0.6.0), not a workspace member; 19+21 tests; serves all activity names from main.rs SERVED_ACTIVITIES.
Stage-contract canon (scout-report, dev-report, review-report) — codegen-subset clean, landed at c25ceeb8.
The rhai ancestor: enrichment pattern, prompt budgets, and norn profiles proven here; retired from aion use once dispatch lands (stays for Meridian until their migration).

constraints.text: Deterministic workflow code performs no filesystem reads, no ledger parsing, and no reference resolution: dispatcher activities resolve roadmap/decisions/checklist/stories references and assemble the brief context before any workflow logic sees it (P5).

Every cap, backoff, deadline, and concurrency limit is a required input field, schema-enforced with additionalProperties: false; the engine and the workflows impose no bound of their own (ADR-001, ADR-003).
enrich_brief is append-only: it refuses to alter any authored field (deep-compares the authored subset before writing) and fails terminally on divergence rather than silently overwriting.

Stage sessions are deterministic and disjoint: '<branch>' for dev (existing), '<branch>-scout' for scout, '<branch>-review' for review. The reviewer NEVER shares the dev session — fresh eyes are the point of the stage (REVIEW.md).
Activity failure taxonomy: can't-execute is terminal failure; expected negative outcomes are successful results carrying data (P3). No retries of broken environments.
The family deploys as a unit (shared content hash, child type = entry module name); all archives rebuilt and redeployed together on any change.
Package stage-contract schemas are byte-identical to docs/design-system/schemas/ canon, enforced by a drift gate that runs with the test suite.

The outer arc's live-proven CLI contracts are immutable in this cluster: request_review argv (branch positional first, --as Meridian), land = git commit in worktree then yg merge from repo_root, single review_verdict signal (ADR-006).
Hermetic tests run under gleeunit's sequential runner (exclusive PATH is VM-global); e2e suites never run concurrently with gleam builds in the same checkout.
